page.title: Ocean Club Marbella by Yacht: Anchor Off, Tender In, the Day That Works

data.meta_description: Arrive at Ocean Club Marbella by yacht from Puerto Banús. Anchor off, tender in, and enjoy VIP access. Prices from €749 for 2 hours. Book now.
data.summary: Learn how to arrive at Ocean Club Marbella by yacht, anchor off the beach, and use a tender to get ashore. Prices start at €749 for 2 hours from Puerto Banús.
key_facts: Ocean Club Marbella is located on the Golden Mile, near Puerto Banús.
All charters depart from Puerto Banús, just 5 minutes from the club.
Anchor off the beach and use the tender (inflatable boat) to reach shore.
Prices from €749 for 2 hours (Tier A boats: Astondoa 40, Azimut 39).
Skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, and insurance included in every charter.
Maximum 12 guests on most boats (check individual boat capacity).
No need to queue for taxis or walk from the marina – direct sea access.

faq.q: How do I get from the yacht to Ocean Club Marbella?
faq.a: You anchor the yacht a short distance off the beach, then use the boat's tender (a small inflatable dinghy) to ferry you and your guests directly to the shore. The tender is included with every charter and is operated by your skipper.

faq.q: Which boats are best for arriving at Ocean Club Marbella?
faq.a: Any of our boats can anchor off the beach. The Astondoa 40 (€749/2h) and Azimut 39 (€749/2h) are popular for smaller groups. For larger parties, the Mangusta 80 (€4,719/4h) or Ferretti 94 (pricing on request) offer more space and luxury.

faq.q: Do I need to book a table at Ocean Club Marbella in advance?
faq.a: Yes, we recommend booking a table at Ocean Club Marbella separately, especially in summer. The yacht charter gets you there in style, but the club itself requires a reservation for dining or sunbed access.

faq.q: How long does it take to sail from Puerto Banús to Ocean Club Marbella?
faq.a: Ocean Club Marbella is located on the Golden Mile, just 5–10 minutes by boat from Puerto Banús. A 2-hour charter gives you plenty of time to anchor, enjoy the club, and return.

faq.q: What is included in the yacht charter price?
faq.a: Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, and safety equipment. Spanish IVA (21%) is also included. You only need to bring your swimwear and sunscreen.

faq.q: Can I anchor anywhere near Ocean Club Marbella?
faq.a: You can anchor off the beach in designated areas. The skipper will know the best spot based on wind and sea conditions. You must not anchor in swimming zones or too close to other vessels.

faq.q: What if the weather is bad on the day of my charter?
faq.a: If conditions are unsafe for anchoring or the tender transfer, we will reschedule your charter at no extra cost. Safety is our priority. Contact us via WhatsApp to discuss alternatives.

faq.q: How many guests can I bring on a yacht to Ocean Club Marbella?
faq.a: Capacity varies by boat: the Astondoa 40 takes 9 guests, the Azimut 39 takes 11, and most larger yachts (Mangusta 80, Ferretti 94) take up to 12. Check the individual boat page for exact numbers.

data.body_html:
Arriving at Ocean Club Marbella by yacht is the most stylish and practical way to visit this iconic beach club. Instead of queuing for taxis or walking along the Golden Mile, you anchor off the beach and take the tender ashore. It's seamless, VIP, and surprisingly affordable — prices start at €749 for 2 hours on a Tier A boat like the Astondoa 40. Here's how it works.

Why Arrive by Yacht at Ocean Club Marbella?

Ocean Club Marbella sits on the Golden Mile, between Marbella and Puerto Banús. The club is famous for its sunbeds, restaurant, and pool parties. Arriving by yacht elevates the experience: you avoid traffic, park for free (on the water), and make an entrance that turns heads. The tender ride from your anchored yacht to the beach takes less than two minutes.

Which Boats Can Anchor Off Ocean Club Marbella?

Any of our boats can anchor off the beach. For a small group, the Astondoa 40 (€749/2h) or Azimut 39 (€749/2h) are perfect. Both are 12.5m, carry up to 11 guests, and include a skipper who handles the anchoring and tender. For larger parties, the Mangusta 80 (€4,719/4h, includes a jet ski) or Ferretti 94 (pricing on request) offer more deck space and luxury.

How the Tender Works

Every yacht comes with a tender — a small inflatable boat with an outboard engine. Your skipper will anchor the main yacht a safe distance from the beach (typically 50–100 metres out, depending on depth and swell). The tender is then lowered or launched, and you and your guests ride it to the shore. The skipper will help you disembark and can return to the yacht if needed. The tender is included in the charter price.

What to Expect at Ocean Club Marbella

Ocean Club Marbella offers sunbeds, a pool, a restaurant, and a lively atmosphere. You can book a table or sunbed in advance (separate from the yacht charter). The club is open from May to October, with peak season in July and August. Expect temperatures of 28–35°C. Drinks and food are paid for on-site — your yacht charter includes drinks and snacks on board, so you can have a pre-club drink on the yacht before heading ashore.

Sample Itinerary: 4-Hour Charter to Ocean Club

Here's a typical 4-hour itinerary:

  • 12:00 – Depart Puerto Banús on your yacht (e.g., Azimut 39).
  • 12:10 – Anchor off Ocean Club Marbella. Enjoy drinks and snacks on board.
  • 12:30 – Take the tender ashore. Spend 2–3 hours at the club (sunbeds, lunch, swimming).
  • 15:30 – Tender back to the yacht. Cruise along the coast towards Cabopino or Estepona.
  • 16:00 – Return to Puerto Banús.

This works perfectly with a Tier A boat at €749/2h or €2,299/8h — you can extend the charter to include more cruising.

What's Included in Every Charter

All our charters include: licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, and safety equipment. Spanish IVA (21%) is included in the price. You only need to bring swimwear, sunscreen, and a towel. The tender is also included — no hidden fees.

Booking Your Yacht to Ocean Club Marbella

To book, contact us via WhatsApp or use the booking form on our site. We recommend booking at least 48 hours in advance, especially in summer. You can also book a table at Ocean Club Marbella separately — we can help with recommendations. Prices for Tier A boats start at €749/2h, Tier B (Mangusta 80) from €4,719/4h, and other boats on request.

Frequently Asked Questions

How do I get from the yacht to Ocean Club Marbella?

You anchor the yacht a short distance off the beach, then use the boat's tender (a small inflatable dinghy) to ferry you and your guests directly to the shore. The tender is included with every charter and is operated by your skipper.

Which boats are best for arriving at Ocean Club Marbella?

Any of our boats can anchor off the beach. The Astondoa 40 (€749/2h) and Azimut 39 (€749/2h) are popular for smaller groups. For larger parties, the Mangusta 80 (€4,719/4h) or Ferretti 94 (pricing on request) offer more space and luxury.

Do I need to book a table at Ocean Club Marbella in advance?

Yes, we recommend booking a table at Ocean Club Marbella separately, especially in summer. The yacht charter gets you there in style, but the club itself requires a reservation for dining or sunbed access.

How long does it take to sail from Puerto Banús to Ocean Club Marbella?

Ocean Club Marbella is located on the Golden Mile, just 5–10 minutes by boat from Puerto Banús. A 2-hour charter gives you plenty of time to anchor, enjoy the club, and return.

What is included in the yacht charter price?

Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, and safety equipment. Spanish IVA (21%) is also included. You only need to bring your swimwear and sunscreen.

Can I anchor anywhere near Ocean Club Marbella?

You can anchor off the beach in designated areas. The skipper will know the best spot based on wind and sea conditions. You must not anchor in swimming zones or too close to other vessels.

What if the weather is bad on the day of my charter?

If conditions are unsafe for anchoring or the tender transfer, we will reschedule your charter at no extra cost. Safety is our priority. Contact us via WhatsApp to discuss alternatives.

How many guests can I bring on a yacht to Ocean Club Marbella?

Capacity varies by boat: the Astondoa 40 takes 9 guests, the Azimut 39 takes 11, and most larger yachts (Mangusta 80, Ferretti 94) take up to 12. Check the individual boat page for exact numbers.
